XB4GS9ShBRE.root_cause_summary: Candidate_1's 0.6m forward-projection carrot changed the Phase 2 stair traversal trajectory, causing the centroid-bypass path (paused=8→Phase 2) to exit the stair at a frontier-poor second-floor position: 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found' fires at floor_step=1 immediately after SUCCESS at paused_step=14, versus candidate_0's 280+ exploration steps after a deeper traversal with three paused_step resets. The binding failure for candidate_0 (false-positive STOP at step 499, DTG=0.131m) remains unaddressed but is masked by candidate_1's regression to never_saw_target (DTG=0.683m) caused by the forward projection.
key_evidence: candidate_1: T6_CENTROID_BYPASS fires at paused=8; paused_step 9-13 all PENDING in_stair_map=True; SUCCESS at paused_step=14 in_stair_map=False — zero paused_step resets (robot never moved >0.2m toward 0.6m carrot), indicating a short traversal exiting stair at a boundary-edge position

candidate_0: paused_step cycled 11→19→0→1→0→SUCCESS@1 — three resets confirming robot moved >0.2m three times during Phase 2, traversing deeper through stair and exiting at a frontier-rich position enabling 280+ explore steps
candidate_1 step 219: floor_step=1, initialize mode, immediately 'In all floors, no unexplored stairs or frontiers found, stopping.' — second floor frontier supply is zero after the shorter traversal
candidate_1 NOQUIT rescues at steps 231 and 244 both immediately fail with same no-frontier message; episode ends at step 257 with DTG=0.683m vs candidate_0's DTG=0.131m
candidate_1 reinits=4 (vs candidate_0 reinits=3); stair_runs=0 (vs multiple stair phases in candidate_0 mode sequence)
candidate_1 failure class: floor_confusion/never_saw_target vs candidate_0's false_positive — the regression erased the DTG=0.131m bed approach

candidate_outcomes.candidate_0: T6 baseline (0.4m pullback). Centroid bypass fires at paused=8; three paused_step resets during Phase 2 (robot moves >0.2m each time) → SUCCESS at paused_step=1 at frontier-rich stair exit. 280+ explore steps (199-479); DTG=0.131m reached. Passive detection at step 482 → second stair climb → floor switch at step 491 → false_positive STOP at step 499. SR=0.8.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_1: 0.6m forward projection. Centroid bypass fires at paused=8; paused_step 9-14 with zero resets → SUCCESS at paused_step=14 at frontier-poor stair exit. Immediate frontier exhaustion (floor_step=1 initialize). NOQUIT rescues at steps 231, 244 both fail. Episode ends step 257, DTG=0.683m. never_saw_target. SR=0.7 — regression from candidate_0.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_4: BFS snap + stale-state fix. SUCCESS at paused_step=20 (same as 0/1). Frontier exhaustion at step 213 (3 steps earlier). Same structural failure. Episode ends at step 251. never_saw_target. SR=0.6 overall.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_8: GUARD_STEPS=35. GUARD_HOLDING from paused_step=12 through 30 (all in_stair_map=False) — 23 extra steps in Phase 2 stair limbo. Agent exits to climb_stair_initialize at floor_step=385, navigate at step 386. false_positive at step ~419. SR=0.6 overall.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_9: DP9 carrot pullback 0.5m toward robot. Phase 2 waypoint changed; paused_step reset 3× during ascent (robot moved >0.2m each time). SUCCESS at paused_step=1 with different stair exit position yielding rich frontier supply. ~280 steps of exploration (steps 199–479). DTG reached 0.131m. Passive detection at step 482 → second stair climb → SUCCESS at paused_step=2 → floor_step=0 at step 491. false_positive at step 499. SR=0.8 overall.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_10: DP9 connected-component validated carrot. XB4GS9ShBRE is NOT a target scene for candidate_10. Behavioral fingerprint identical to candidate_9 (WARNING confirmed). Same paused_step reset pattern during ascent, same DTG=0.131m, same passive detection sequence at step 482, same second floor switch at step 491, same false_positive at step 499. The connected-component check had zero effect here — XB4GS9ShBRE's Phase 2 carrot was already on the robot's navmesh component following candidate_9's pullback. SR=0.8 (unchanged).
candidate_outcomes.candidate_0_T6: T6 baseline carries T5 best (DP9 carrot pullback 0.4m + all prior fixes). Behavioral fingerprint identical to T5 candidates 9 and 10 (same DTG=0.131m, same paused_step reset pattern, same passive detection at step 482, same second floor switch at step 491, same false_positive at step 499). Zero behavioral change from T5 c10. SR=0.8 (unchanged).
candidate_outcomes.candidate_0_T5: Baseline T4. SUCCESS at paused_step=20, floor_step=0 at step 198. Post-switch: only 2 frontiers at 0.2m and 2.2m. Frontier exhaustion at step 216 → 'In all floors, no unexplored stairs or frontiers found, stopping.' NOQUIT rescues at steps 228 and 241. Five floor_reinits. Episode ends at step 254. never_saw_target. SR=0.7.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_9_T5: DP9 carrot pullback 0.5m toward robot. Phase 2 waypoint changed; paused_step reset 3× during ascent. SUCCESS at paused_step=1 with different stair exit position yielding rich frontier supply. ~280 steps of exploration. DTG reached 0.131m. Passive detection at step 482 → second stair climb → SUCCESS at paused_step=2 → floor_step=0 at step 491. false_positive at step 499. SR=0.8 overall.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_10_T5: DP9 connected-component validated carrot. XB4GS9ShBRE not a target scene. Behavioral fingerprint identical to T5 c9 (WARNING confirmed). Same DTG=0.131m, same mode sequence, same false_positive at step 499. Connected-component check had zero effect — Phase 2 carrot already on robot's component after c9 pullback. SR=0.8 (unchanged).

ruled_out_reasoning.DP7: dp7_empty=0/0 in both candidates; zero LLM calls — LLM parsing has no causal role.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP5: No LLM calls; goal binding not invoked.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP2: No LLM calls; trigger threshold irrelevant.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP12: Floor switches driven by passive detection and no-frontier paths, not DP12 interval gate.
ruled_out_reasoning.premature_stair_climb_success_guard: paused_step=20 already satisfies MIN_STAIR_STEPS=15 in T5 c1; guard is counterproductive (T5 c8 GUARD=35 caused floor_step=385 wasted budget).
ruled_out_reasoning.post_floor_switch_frontier_seeding_radiu: Candidate_1 demonstrates frontier supply is determined by stair exit position, not seeding radius: both candidates had NOQUIT rescue access but c1 still found no frontiers.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP9_phase2_connected_component_validatio: T5 c10 and T6 c0 confirmed bit-for-bit identical to T5 c9 — Phase 2 carrot already on robot's navmesh component from pullback.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP9_phase2_forward_projection_0.6m: Candidate_1 definitively falsified: 0.6m forward projection caused frontier-poor stair exit and regression from DTG=0.131m to DTG=0.683m. The 0.4m pullback must be restored.

open_questions: What object was detected as 'bed' at step 499 (DTG=0.131m) after the second floor switch at step 491 — sofa, mattress, or another bed-like object on the third floor?

Would a guard that suppresses STOP when mode=initialize AND floor_step<20 prevent the false-positive at step 499 with zero risk of blocking true positives (true positives require the agent to have explored the floor, not be in a fresh initialize window)?

Is the second floor switch at step 491 ascending to the correct bed floor or to a third floor — if the bed is on floor 2 and the second switch goes to floor 3, suppressing passive detection at step 482 would be the correct fix.

causal_graph.DP9_forward_0.6m\u2192shorter_Phase2_tra: Confirmed by candidate_1: paused_step 9-14 with zero resets vs candidate_0's three resets; immediate no-frontier at floor_step=1
causal_graph.passive_stair_detection_step482\u2192sec: Confirmed in candidate_0; unchanged by candidate_1 which regressed before reaching this point

decision_point_attribution.description: In candidate_1, step 214 is when stair SUCCESS fires at paused_step=14 after only 6 Phase-2 steps with zero paused_step resets. The robot exits the stair at a boundary-edge position with no second-floor frontiers. This contrasts with candidate_0's SUCCESS at paused_step=1 after three resets (deeper traversal placing robot at a frontier-rich position). The stair exit position at step 214 determines whether the remaining 285 steps can find the bed.

contrastive_analysis.note: Candidate_0 reached DTG=0.131m (best ever for this scene) but failed with false_positive. Candidate_1 regressed to DTG=0.683m with never_saw_target due to frontier-poor stair exit. The structural difference: Phase 2 carrot direction (backward 0.4m vs forward 0.6m) determines how deeply the robot traverses the stair and therefore where it exits on the second floor. The 0.4m pullback is the correct direction and must be restored.

behavioral_fingerprints.candidate_0_T6: initialize(1-12)→navigate(13-14)→explore(15-50)→navigate(51-51)→explore(52-121)→get_close_to_stair(122-149)→look_up(150-...[paused_step_resets 11→19→0→1→0→SUCCESS@1→extensive_explore(199-479)→passive_detection(482)→climb_stair(488-491)→initialize→false_positive(499)]  [IDENTICAL to T5 c9 and T5 c10]

telemetry_insights.llm_parse_rate_trend: N/A (0 LLM calls in all candidates for this scene)

hypothesis_outcome.hypothesis: DP9 forward projection 0.6m into stair body to commit agent to ascending past the first tread.
hypothesis_outcome.assessment: Hypothesis falsified. The 0.6m forward projection caused candidate_1 to exit the stair at a frontier-poor position (zero paused_step resets vs three in candidate_0), regressing from DTG=0.131m to DTG=0.683m and from false_positive to never_saw_target. Actual SR delta = -0.1. The 0.4m pullback must be restored; the false-positive STOP guard (suppress STOP during initialize mode when floor_step<20) remains the sole unaddressed target for XB4GS9ShBRE.

qyAac8rV8Zk.root_cause_summary: Candidate_0 (T6 baseline) SOLVED this scene for the first time across all tracks: the centroid bypass (paused=8→Phase 2) combined with the 0.4m carrot pullback placed the Phase 2 waypoint at a robot-adjacent navigable position, enabling downstair traversal at step ~399 and SUCCESS at step 415 with DTG=0.094m. Candidate_1's 0.6m forward projection moved the Phase 2 carrot back into the disconnected navmesh region, reinstating the pre-T6 failure mode exactly: min_dis_to_downstair oscillates at 160-166, Reach_stair_centroid stays False throughout, and the frontier is disabled twice without any floor switch.
key_evidence: candidate_0: SUCCESS at step 415, reinits=2, stair_runs=1, DTG=0.094m — first ever success for this scene across T2, T4, T5, T6 tracks; confirmed by 'Success rate of Scene qyAac8rV8Zk: 100.00000%'
candidate_0 log: Mode=navigate at step 399 with Stair_flag=2 (downstairs mode active) — agent successfully navigating downstairs on the way to couch
candidate_1: min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 166→160 throughout (identical to prior-track failures in T2/T5); Reach_stair_centroid: False on every logged step

candidate_1: Frontier [-1.22463054 -8.19236453] disabled twice identically to prior failure pattern ('disabled due to no movement or reaching start', then 'Pointnav policy stopped. Disabling stair frontier'); episode ends step 239, DTG=2.075m
candidate_1 failure tag: never_saw_target_did_not_travel_stairs_likely_infeasible — exact same class as all prior-track failures, confirming candidate_1 nullified the T6 baseline fix

The centroid bypass fires at paused=8 in both candidates; with 0.4m pullback (c0) the Phase 2 carrot lands near robot on connected navmesh; with 0.6m forward projection (c1) the carrot is pushed into the disconnected stair region

candidate_outcomes.candidate_0: T6 baseline (centroid bypass + 0.4m pullback). First ever success for this scene. Stair_flag=2 (downstairs) active at step 399. SUCCESS at step 415, reinits=2, stair_runs=1, DTG=0.094m. The centroid bypass forced Phase 2 after 8 paused steps of failed Phase 1 centroid approach; the 0.4m pullback placed the Phase 2 carrot at a robot-adjacent navigable position enabling eventual downstair traversal.
candidate_outcomes.candidate_1: 0.6m forward projection. Reinstates pre-T6 failure: min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 166→160 (navmesh boundary wall), Reach_stair_centroid: False throughout, frontier [-1.22463054 -8.19236453] disabled twice. Episode ends step 239, DTG=2.075m. never_saw_target. SR=0.7 — REGRESSION from candidate_0 success.

ruled_out_reasoning.DP7: dp7_empty=0/0 in candidate_1; candidate_0 had 0/2 (all parsed at rate 1.00). LLM parsing not a factor.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP5: Parse rate 1.00; prior T2 fix shifted centroid to equally inaccessible coordinate — insufficient standalone.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP2: LLM calls made and parsed in candidate_0; trigger threshold not suppressing guidance.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP12: floor_reinits=1-2; agent stuck in stair approach, not exhausting same-floor frontiers via DP12 path.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP1: Frontier scoring correctly selected same-floor frontiers during explore(59-163); failure is in stair centroid approach, not ranking.
ruled_out_reasoning.premature_stair_climb_success_guard: Addresses upstair is_robot_in_stair_map_fast logic only; qyAac8rV8Zk fails on downstair centroid approach (get_close_to_stair). T5 c0/c1 showed logs identical — structurally off-path.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP9_phase2_forward_projection_0.6m: Candidate_1 definitively falsified: 0.6m forward projection reinstated the pre-T6 failure mode. The 0.4m pullback is the solved mechanism and must be preserved at all costs.

open_questions: What specific trajectory did candidate_0 take between steps 164-399 to achieve downstair traversal — did the centroid bypass fire multiple times, or was a single Phase 2 engagement (0.4m pullback) sufficient to place the carrot at the navigable boundary of the stair?

Is there a paused_step threshold lower than 8 for the downstairs case that would trigger Phase 2 earlier, avoiding more Phase 1 futile centroid approach steps and increasing the step budget for actual downstair exploration?

Could the solution for candidate_0 be replicated reliably: does the stair traversal happen because the Phase 2 carrot at 0.4m lands exactly at the navigable pixel nearest the stair centroid, or is there a probabilistic element based on approach angle?

causal_graph.centroid_bypass_paused=8 + pullback_0.4m: Confirmed by candidate_0 success with Stair_flag=2 active at step 399
causal_graph.forward_projection_0.6m \u2192 Phase2_ca: Confirmed by candidate_1 reinstating exact pre-T6 failure pattern
causal_graph.candidate_0_solves_qyAac8rV8Zk \u2192 ca: The causal direction is pullback (toward robot) not forward projection — this is now a controlled experiment with a single variable

decision_point_attribution.description: Step 164 marks entry into get_close_to_stair in both candidates. In candidate_0, the centroid bypass at paused=8 forced Phase 2 with the 0.4m pullback carrot — eventually enabling traversal success at ~step 399 and episode success at step 415. In candidate_1, Phase 2 fires with the 0.6m forward carrot pushed into the disconnected region, so min_dis_to_downstair never converges and the frontier is disabled twice without any floor switch.

contrastive_analysis.note: Candidate_0 is the sole success for this scene across all tracks. The single variable difference between success and failure is Phase 2 carrot offset direction: 0.4m toward robot (candidate_0, SUCCESS) vs 0.6m into stair body (candidate_1, FAIL). Identical mode sequence prefix, identical centroid target, identical centroid bypass threshold — this is a clean controlled experiment confirming that pullback direction is essential for disconnected downstair centroids.

telemetry_insights.llm_parse_rate_trend: stable (candidate_0: 1.00 with 2 LLM calls; candidate_1: N/A with 0 LLM calls)

hypothesis_outcome.hypothesis: DP9 forward projection 0.6m into stair body to commit agent to ascending past the first tread.
hypothesis_outcome.assessment: Hypothesis falsified. The 0.6m forward projection broke the T6 baseline's solved mechanism: candidate_0's 0.4m pullback enabled the first-ever success (DTG=0.094m, step 415); candidate_1's forward projection reinstated the pre-T6 failure (DTG=2.075m, step 239, min_dis oscillating at 160-166). Actual SR delta = -0.1. The pullback direction is the correct and confirmed fix; forward projection is contraindicated for navmesh-disconnected stair centroids.

q3zU7Yy5E5s.root_cause_summary: The upstair centroid [-2.12027027, 3.27567568] lies in a navmesh-disconnected component. Candidate_5's floor.py __init_subclass__ injection with a ±50px box check at gcts_step=0 produced a byte-identical behavioral fingerprint to all prior candidates (c0/c2/c3/c4), confirming the [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] tag never fired with centroid_navigable=False. The most likely cause: the ±50px box expands into the connected navmesh component boundary near the disconnected island, causing the check to return True and Phase 1 to proceed normally — the same dormant outcome as all gcts_streak-based approaches. All six T6 interventions targeting this scene have been structurally ineffective; the fix requires a strict single-pixel centroid check at the exact centroid coordinates, or injection at the correct upstream call site where the upstair/downstair selection is made.
key_evidence: candidate_5: behavioral fingerprint BYTE-IDENTICAL to candidate_0/c2/c3/c4 (WARNING confirmed) — [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] tag never appeared in logs, floor.py injection produced zero observable effect
candidate_5: look_for_downstair(70-81) window = 12 steps, unchanged across all 6 T6 candidates — no Phase 1 entry point has been successfully intercepted
candidate_5: DTG=0.9927m, stair_runs=2, reinits=3, steps=499 — identical to candidate_3; zero SR delta vs candidate_3/c4
candidate_5: same DP1 frontier sequence [-0.95,0.72]→[-0.36,0.07]→[-0.35,-0.96]→[0.2,-1.71] as all prior candidates — exploration path completely unchanged
candidate_5: overall SR=0.9, identical to candidate_3/c4 — floor.py __init_subclass__ injection via ±50px box contributed nothing
Falsifiability check from hypothesis required zero Reach_stair_centroid:False before redirect — instead same failure pattern observed (stair_runs=2 with no floor traversal)

±50px box logic: disconnected island at [-2.12, 3.28] is geometrically adjacent to connected component; 50px (~1.25m at standard resolution) likely encompasses navigable pixels in neighboring region, causing centroid_navigable=True

candidate_outcomes.candidate_4: Downstair redirect after gcts_streak=10 early disable. BYTE-IDENTICAL fingerprint to candidate_0/c2/c3 (WARNING confirmed). stair_runs=2, DTG=0.9962m, steps=499. Redirect never executed. SR=0.9.

Hypothesis falsified for this scene.

candidate_outcomes.candidate_0_T5: Baseline T4. Phase 1 Phase 1 centroid failures, episode terminates step ~381. DTG ~2.84m.

Centroid shifted to [-2.10394737, 3.27368421] (equally inaccessible). 75-step regression (381→456), soft_spl degradation. SR=0.7.

No [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] tag in logs. DTG=0.9927m, stair_runs=2, steps=499. The ±50px box almost certainly returned centroid_navigable=True by finding navigable pixels in the adjacent connected component — Phase 1 proceeded normally, identical to baseline. SR=0.9 (unchanged). Hypothesis definitively falsified.

ruled_out_reasoning.DP7: dp7_empty=0/1 across all 6 candidates; LLM parse rate=1.00. LLM parsing not a factor.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP5: Parse rate 1.00 in all T6 candidates; goal binding irrelevant when navmesh disconnection is structural.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP2: One LLM call made and parsed per candidate.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP12: floor_reinits=3 driven by no-frontier path, not DP12 interval gate.
ruled_out_reasoning.premature_stair_climb_success_guard: Reach_stair_centroid never True for upstair Phase 1 — Phase 3 never entered. Guard structurally unreachable.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP9_phase2_pullback_alone: T5 c9/c10 and T6 c0/c3/c4/c5 all achieve DTG~1.0m — Phase 2 improvements saturated.
ruled_out_reasoning.DP9_phase2_connected_component_validatio: T5 c10 bit-for-bit identical to T5 c9; Phase 2 carrot already on robot component.
ruled_out_reasoning.Phase2_BFS_snap_to_nearest_navigable_poi: T6 c2 BFS snap caused SR regression without improving this scene.
ruled_out_reasoning.early_gcts_disable_gcts_streak_10: candidate_3: IDENTICAL fingerprint to baseline (WARNING confirmed). Native stall fires at step 81; N=10 fires at most step 79 — dormant.
ruled_out_reasoning.gcts_streak_based_downstair_redirect_any: candidate_4: BYTE-IDENTICAL fingerprint to c0/c2/c3 — redirect never executed regardless of attached logic.
ruled_out_reasoning.floor_py_init_subclass_injection_with_50: candidate_5: BYTE-IDENTICAL fingerprint to all prior candidates; [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] never appeared. The ±50px box search radius is too coarse — the disconnected island is geometrically adjacent to the connected component, so the expanded box finds navigable pixels and returns centroid_navigable=True, allowing Phase 1 to proceed normally. The injection either executed correctly and returned True, or the __init_subclass__ hook was not invoked on the correct call path. Either way the result is identical to baseline.

open_questions: What exact pixel does [-2.12027027, 3.27567568] map to via _xy_to_px, and how close (in pixels) is the nearest True pixel in om._navigable_map? If within 50px (~1.25m), the ±50px box returns True and is useless as a disconnection detector.

Where exactly does the __init_subclass__ hook execute in the call chain? Does it fire before or after the stair centroid is committed for Phase 1? If it fires at class definition time rather than at gcts_step=0, the injection point is semantically wrong.

What is the correct call site in ascent_policy.py or llm_planner.py where the upstair vs downstair routing decision is made? Injecting at _navigate_stair_if_unexplored_floor or the stair-mode entry (look_for_upstair/downstair) would be a semantically unambiguous point.

Is there a way to inspect mc._has_up_stair and mc._stair_map at episode initialization time to pre-disable the upstair centroid before any gcts call is ever made? A hook in on_episode_start or post_reset that checks centroid navigability and marks it disabled would fire before step 70.

In the second stair run (after explore 83-178), which stair centroid does the agent attempt? If it attempts the upstair centroid again (already disabled → re-enabled by reinit) rather than the downstair centroid, the second run also fails for the same reason.

causal_graph.upstair_centroid_disconnected\u2192Reach: Confirmed across all 6 T6 candidates and all prior T5 candidates
causal_graph.native_stall_fires_at_step_12\u2192gcts_: Confirmed by IDENTICAL fingerprint c0/c2/c3/c4; look_for_downstair(70-81) preserved across all
causal_graph.gcts_streak_based_trigger\u2192downstair: Definitively confirmed by c3 and c4 WARNING
causal_graph.50px_box_check_finds_adjacent_navigable_: Inferred from c5 BYTE-IDENTICAL fingerprint and absence of [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] tag; geometrically plausible given island proximity to connected component
causal_graph.all_six_T6_interventions_structurally_do: New conclusion from c5: the injection point must be upstream of Phase 1 entry, at stair centroid selection or episode initialization time

decision_point_attribution.description: Step 70 marks Phase 1 gcts entry (look_for_downstair). All six T6 interventions (c2: BFS snap; c3: gcts_streak=10 disable; c4: gcts_streak downstair redirect; c5: floor.py ±50px box check) produce byte-identical mode sequences, confirming that no intervention has successfully intercepted the Phase 1 entry point. The correct fix requires injection either at episode initialization (before step 1) to pre-disable the disconnected centroid, or at the stair-mode routing decision point in ascent_policy.py where upstair vs downstair is chosen — not at gcts_step=0 within an already-committed Phase 1 attempt.

contrastive_analysis.note: No successes across all 6 T6 candidates. Five of six (c0/c2/c3/c4/c5) are byte-identical (DTG~1.0m, stair_runs=2, steps=499). Only c1 differs (stair_runs=1, DTG=2.815m) and that is a regression. The binding constraint — Phase 1 centroid navmesh disconnection — has resisted all gcts_streak-based (c3, c4), BFS snap (c2), and floor.py injection (c5) approaches. A structural change at episode initialization time or the upstream stair routing decision is the only remaining untested approach.

hypothesis_outcome.hypothesis: q3zU7Yy5E5s upstair centroid [-2.12027027, 3.27567568] lies in a navmesh-disconnected component. A proactive navmesh check in floor.py via __init_subclass__ injection fires at gcts_step=0 by checking om._navigable_map within a ±50px box around the upstair centroid; if no navigable pixel found, immediately call mc._disable_stair_and_reset_state() and force climb_direction=2 before Phase 1 begins.
hypothesis_outcome.assessment: Hypothesis definitively falsified for q3zU7Yy5E5s. The behavioral fingerprint is byte-identical to all five prior candidates (WARNING confirmed), [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] never appeared in logs, and overall SR delta vs candidate_4 is 0.0. The ±50px box check is the most likely point of failure: the disconnected island at [-2.12, 3.28] is geometrically adjacent to the connected navmesh region, so the expanded ±50px search finds a navigable pixel in the neighboring connected area and returns centroid_navigable=True — identical to Phase 1 proceeding normally. The floor.py __init_subclass__ injection approach with a coarse box check is no more effective than the gcts_streak-based approaches in c3/c4; both are structurally dormant for this scene's navmesh topology. The correct fix requires either a strict single-pixel check at the exact centroid coordinates, or injection at the upstream stair routing decision point (before Phase 1 is ever committed), not within the gcts loop after Phase 1 has begun.
